title: Bluebells
content:
Where the bluebells and the wind are,
        Fairies in a ring I spied,
     And I heard a little linnet
        Singing near beside.

     Where the primrose and the dew are - 
        Soon were sped the fairies all:
     Only now the green turf freshens,
        And the linnets call.
meaning:
Walter De La Mare's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "Bluebells"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
